primary_accounting_issue: Définition du critère SPPI (« solely payments of principal and interest ») pour la classification des actifs financiers selon IFRS 9

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the stated precedence rule by selecting the IFRS standard that directly governs the SPPI classification criterion for financial assets; no lower-precedence overlapping governing document addresses the same eligibility question more directly.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty: IFRS 9 directly contains the SPPI test and the related classification outcomes for financial assets.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly define principal and interest, explain the SPPI assessment, and link that assessment to the classification categories for financial assets.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs support the analysis by defining a financial asset as a contractual right and clarifying the contractual nature of financial instruments.

peripheral_authority.reason: This authority addresses service concession consideration and only incidentally refers to IFRS 9 measurement categories rather than the SPPI definition itself.
This authority relates to employee benefits and plan assets, not the SPPI classification test for financial assets.
This authority concerns contingent consideration in business combinations and is not relevant to identifying peer approaches for the SPPI criterion.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest and that are held to collect contractual cash flows
Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest and that are held both to collect contractual cash flows and to sell
Financial assets that do not meet the SPPI condition or do not fall within an eligible business model

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies amortised cost as a top-level classification outcome for financial assets that meet the SPPI condition and are held to collect contractual cash flows.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through other comprehensive income as a top-level classification outcome for financial assets that meet the SPPI condition and are held both to collect cash flows and to sell.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through profit or loss as the residual top-level classification outcome when the SPPI condition is not met or the asset is not within an eligible business model.
